mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales momentum signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms; when RSAFS has a positive 3-month growth rate, the S&P 500 earns positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest value 403929.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth has nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if positive RSAFS momentum periods do not have higher mean returns than negative momentum periods.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment threshold marks recessionary labor slack that depresses consumption and earnings expectations; when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, the S&P 500 produces negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest value 8.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE-above-8.0 episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations, or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is pinned near zero while CPI inflation is not accelerating sharply, low discount rates dominate inflation concerns and support equity valuation expansion over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth below 4.0%
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.16 as of t-1mo
fact:CPIAUCSL latest value 228.723 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 and contained-CPI trigger has a negative 60-day return mean, or if an interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum captures the real-side earnings cycle; when INDPRO has positive 3-month growth, improving capacity utilization and operating leverage drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest value 98.0398 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of triggered observations, or if its rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts breakdown signals weakening durable-goods demand, credit creation, and household balance-sheet confidence; when HOUST crosses below 800 thousand units, the S&P 500 earns negative returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest value 760.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-800 triggered episodes show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered mean return is not statistically below the unconditional 60-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: Broad demand acceleration requires both quarterly real GDP growth and monthly retail sales growth; when GDPC1 2-quarter growth and RSAFS 3-month growth are both positive, synchronized demand drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth above 0% and RSAFS 3-month growth above 0%
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:RSAFS
fact:GDPC1 latest value 13558.0 as of t-4mo
fact:RSAFS latest value 403929.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth trigger does not outperform either GDPC1-only or RSAFS-only positive-growth signals in out-of-sample 60-day return prediction, or if its average triggered return is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid policy-rate increase tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates faster than earnings expectations adjust; when FEDFUNDS rises more than 50 basis points over 3 months, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses above +0.50 percentage points
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.16 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS tightening triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations, or if the triggered average return is not below the non-triggered average.

mechanism_narrative: Early labor-market repair after a shock is bullish when payrolls expand while unemployment remains elevated, because earnings expectations recover before wage inflation pressure emerges; this interaction predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth above 0% and UNRATE above 7.0%
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:PAYEMS latest value 133245.0 as of t-1mo
fact:UNRATE latest value 8.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-growth and high-UNRATE trigger has nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if it fails to outperform PAYEMS growth alone in walk-forward directional hit rate.
